title: Actaea erythrocarpa
intro: Actaea erythrocarpa, also known as red berries Actaea, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Ranunculaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, as well as potential uses in treating conditions such as fever and rheumatism. Bioactive constituents of Actaea erythrocarpa include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Actaea erythrocarpa is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used to make infusions and ointments.
taxonomy: Actaea erythrocarpa is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It is native to China and is also known as the red-berried unicorn plant.
